name: Mark Kelly
subject: President Trump is calling for me to be hanged.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE ARIZONA DEMOCRATIC PARTY
body:
|
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119793118/584244651/-1397648958?refcode=AU_AZD_FR_TAN_VIP_AC_20251122_AM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjUvMS8xMDU1MTYiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGRmMjg0ODgtYzZjNy1mMDExLTgxOTYtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=t0qIlQ7P2hP6G3K_9M_qZI0lX2vWfvNYESXnDxZfl8Q=)
---
President Trump is calling for me to be hanged. I put out a video, alongside veterans of the military and former members of the intelligence community. It had a simple message: If you are serving in the military, you have an obligation to refuse illegal orders. Trump’s friends in the White House didn’t like that. They accused us of insurrection. At some point, the President heard about it, and he hasn’t stopped posting and reposting about us since — accusing us of sedition, calling for our arrest, calling for us to be hanged. It is no small thing for the President of the United States to call for your execution. Elon Musk called me a traitor. Trump’s advisor, Roger Stone, called for me to be executed. And now Trump himself has called for me to be hanged. As someone whose family has faced political violence before, I know you have to take words like this seriously, and that’s why I’m writing to you. There’s no telling what kind of storm we’re heading into right now. Unfortunately, we need to be prepared for any outcome. | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

And at the center of it... isn't NVIDIA.

It's a **tiny supplier** NVIDIA _cannot live without_.

This firm controls **nearly 7,000 patents** on the technology powering NVIDIA's new _Blackwell_ and _THOR_ Superchips.

Patents so critical, the U.S. government blocked NVIDIA's attempt to buy the company outright - calling the tech _"vital to national security."_

Now insiders like Ken Griffin and Stanley Druckenmiller are dumping NVIDIA shares... and quietly piling into this obscure stock.

And with NVIDIA set to make a major announcement on **December 19th at 5pm** , time is running out.

[Your confidential access has been approved - go here now to unlock the report.](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=4a111f65a536491cb0d85c88c691ae11&_e=UrF8mF11_T4u1UA48E7vH2T5WO7JY8Jzc7DlBlrNOgk5hKQZZskGtcExZ4PCk5waswpW1d_sTfVJTvORS2Xha35Q8jiMix6yD7NKOG973AmR5o0Pdvu4MhfxXNuHEqnAElmiQs3yrVtu1B3y_x67SG4n4twlv_CLWpE8DR7M3N5l3KkqKgtxEZrHBKU0lImqCIDualaCJraC3nvgdXHPxMT0AjYDCJSI7T-ngT4X8sMScTS6UMK20fb29PL6JxsAvkWxxcHwrafWju36Nt-mdk0cqLNqavNbAii2pIJyqT2IQ-jtNKkDqZCLnCTFNkoiLDTID_sgWEwBpRlHHhyNt837mwlYNc7o6jqfqtarTBEySUthACAGf4y3QncBuxc7V57NtPT44ZLYAf4s8DhB4uN_rUVaE0nZ8KhAo3aHnEvrpGlUK2ImyWDupsvijzSG-fAdY5n4tQAWLWNCqnUAM9yEjE-ts4OJgEqb1KmYg1xqjqjx21cJVRyqvOG-RV0PdeEggmyqig8CI4klq_a7eYWgLprxRV4hO25sONnOH80Jiny-vqL85b5ipX3eqNCS)
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

clean_body: It's a tiny supplier NVIDIA cannot live without.| | | | November 22 | | | | | Bloomberg says AI is a $50 trillion revolution. | | | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. This firm controls nearly 7,000 patents on the technology powering NVIDIA's new Blackwell and THOR Superchips. Patents so critical, the U.S. government blocked NVIDIA's attempt to buy the company outright - calling the tech "vital to national security." Now insiders like Ken Griffin and Stanley Druckenmiller are dumping NVIDIA shares... and quietly piling into this obscure stock. And with NVIDIA set to make a major announcement on December 19th at 5pm , time is running out. Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from Your Capital Minds (YCM), an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. subject: Checking in

body:
#
We wanted to take a moment to say **thank you.** Your support—whether it’s your time, energy, or donations—is what keeps Powered by People moving, organizing, and winning.
And because of you and this grassroots team, we’re able to do the work that really matters:
* **Registering voters** so everyone has a voice in shaping their communities;
* **Hosting town halls** where neighbors, advocates, and leaders can come together to share ideas and push for change;
* **Organizing in communities too often left out of the conversation** , making sure no one is forgotten in the fight for a democracy that works for everyone.

Your support doesn’t just make these things possible—**it powers them.** Every action, every donation, every hour you put in helps build a movement strong enough to win in 2026 and beyond.
**With the midterm elections now less than a year away, we’d love to hear from you about why you support Powered by People. Your insights will help us strengthen our movement and ensure that the work we do reflects the passion and priorities of our supporters.**
[**Please take this quick survey to share your thoughts. It won’t take more than a few minutes of your time.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/signup/2025-why-do-you-support-powered-by-people/?source=ema20251122-240481&t=2&akid=240481%2E444211%2EJnaDiE)

Powered by People

name: Abdul El-Sayed
subject: The 4th quarter, the last round, overtime
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Abdul for U
body:
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1001&akid=1342%2E251025%2E02rnQN ]Abdul for US Senate [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/30?refcode=&t=1002&akid=1342%2E251025%2E02rnQN ]Abdul for US Senate

Team,

Yes, you are my team. See, I played a lot of sports growing up, including
lacrosse.

[ A ]A throwback picture of Abdul playing lacrosse

I learned more about leadership on the athletic field than anywhere else.
Teams are special because they show up for each other in the cut. The
“cut” is the 4th quarter, the last round, overtime. When everything is on
the line. Your team shows up for each other, for you.

We’re lining up against the establishment. They’ve got corporate money.
They’ve got connections. They’ve got AIPAC. But we’ve got the best thing
of all: we’ve got you, we’ve got each other, our team.

You’ve already shown up for me once. You’ve joined our team. You didn’t
have to. You chose to, because you believe in what we can do together.

But now, I need to know you’ll be there for me in the cut.
[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/543?c0=em-ms-aes-20251122-1-ak.Yxxkvz&amount=10&refcode=em20251122-1336&t=1004&akid=1342%2E251025%2E02rnQN ]Peter,
could you start a monthly contribution of
any amount to fuel our campaign?

Earning your monthly support means when we’re making decisions in the cut
— whether we can open one more field office, buy one more box of campaign
literature, knock a neighborhood’s doors one more time — I know I can
count on you.

[ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/543?c0=em-ms-aes-20251122-1-ak.Yxxkvz&amount=10&refcode=em20251122-1336&t=1023&akid=1342%2E251025%2E02rnQN ]Donate $10 monthly ››

Teams show up for each other in the cut. [ https://act.abdulformichigan.com/go/543?c0=em-ms-aes-20251122-1-ak.Yxxkvz&amount=10&refcode=em20251122-1336&t=1024&akid=1342%2E251025%2E02rnQN ]Please make your donation
recurring and show up for me when I need you. Because I promise I’ll show
up for you.

Yours for all of us,

Abdul

name: David Hogg
subject: Marjorie Taylor Greene
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
Talk is cheap. How will you vote?
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20251122-1&t=1&akid=2863%2E587540%2E5rYUrt)
Hi Peter, David here.
With all the news about Marjorie Taylor Greene lately, I want to share the story of how I first met her.
In 2019, just a year after the shooting at my high school, I was in D.C. lobbying for gun control. Marjorie Taylor Greene wasn’t in Congress yet, but she was already a far-right troll spreading conspiracy theories online.
She tracked me down as I was walking into the Capitol, recording me, screaming at me, and calling me a coward because I wouldn’t engage. I stayed silent — not just because I was genuinely concerned for my safety, but because I was taught not to feed bullies. And honestly, you’d think a grown woman would realize how absolutely deranged she looked chasing an 18-year-old around the Capitol.
Fast forward to now. You’ve probably seen her sudden breakup with MAGA and her media apology tour trying to rebrand herself as a moderate, serious leader.
For years, her entire political identity was conspiracy theories, white supremacy, and cruelty. And now? She says she’s sorry, claims social media misled her, and wants us to think she no longer believes in the conspiracies she spread for years.
Last week on MSNBC, I was asked if I believe her. Honestly? It's hard to take seriously. A woman who built her career spreading hate and putting people in danger doesn’t just change overnight. It’s likely she’s either angling for a 2028 presidential run or putting on a revenge tour after Trump blocked her Georgia governor bid.
But one thing is undeniable: her constituents are struggling. ACA subsidies are expiring, costs are rising, and working people are feeling the squeeze. She knows it — and she knows staying silent could hurt her politically.
But no matter how she got here, here’s what I said directly to her:
**If Marjorie Taylor Greene wants to work together to figure out how we can save _even just one life_ from gun violence or protect access to healthcare, that is something I’m willing to do. I’ll be the adult here and focus on what matters: stop the senseless death caused by weak gun laws and ensure people don't lose their care because ACA subsidies lapse.**
That's what we need to be focused on, not just the past and the screwed up things that she's done. I wouldn't say that I'm in a position to forgive her for everything she’s said, including that the shooting at my high school didn't happen. But what I care about more than anything is not an apology to me or the other people she's said incredibly insulting things to, but **what she does to show that she's _actually_ changed. And that will be through her votes.**
That applies to every MAGA politician realizing their policies are hurting the people who elected them. Many only act because they’re worried about re-election. But whatever finally gets them to start helping, we can’t wait — action has to start _now._
This is exactly why Leaders We Deserve exists, Peter. We’re helping elect young, progressive leaders who do what’s right for their community, not what’s right for their re-election odds. We’re building a new generation of leadership that doesn’t just talk on cable news — they show up, take hard votes, reject corporate PAC money, and fight like our futures depend on it. Because _they_ do.
**[If you’d like to help build our bench of young progressive leaders, please chip in $5 or anything else to power our work.](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251122_1_mgt.Kv1888&refcode=em20251122-2863&amount=5&t=2&akid=2863%2E587540%2E5rYUrt)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/2160?c0=em_ms_lwd_20251122_1_mgt.Kv1888&amount=5&refcode=em20251122-2863&t=21&akid=2863%2E587540%2E5rYUrt)**
Saying sorry doesn’t save lives. Action does.
— David

name: Chris Deluzio
subject: “Punishable by DEATH!”
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CHRIS DELUZIO FOR CONGRESS
body:
That was Donald Trump’s response to me and my fellow military (and intel) veterans in Congress — all because he didn’t want to hear a basic lesson:

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119793673/584246235/1895761198?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2VhMDk1MC1jOGM3LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=g_D0CSbFXAGh6VV4tOwXgiOkgIonQFPjiqRtBpH19nQ=&emci=a1e84ecc-c4c7-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=e3ea0950-c8c7-f011-8196-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=13185894) **“Punishable by DEATH!”**

That was Donald Trump’s response to me and my fellow military (and intel) veterans in Congress — all because he didn’t want to hear a basic lesson:

_**When you serve our country, your oath to the Constitution comes above all.**_

And he especially didn’t want to hear it from a guy like me who answered the call to serve our nation in uniform — something Donald Trump used his daddy’s wealth and power to avoid during the Vietnam war.

Derek, the basic idea that we would never expect our troops to follow illegal orders is not a controversial one. It’s one that I learned as a plebe in the Naval Academy. It’s one that I trained my sailors and soldiers with.

The fact that this President’s (the Commander in Chief’s) response to that is to call for our death, our hanging, and our arrest, tells me everything I need to know about his respect for our laws.

**Derek, now is the moment for every patriot to reject this call for political violence — and proudly stand up in defense of our Constitution.** [**If you’re with me, I am humbly asking for your support today — I will keep standing up against Trump’s threats and defending the Constitution I swore an oath to protect →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119793675/584246238/320240526?refcode=email_112225_trumpresponse&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2VhMDk1MC1jOGM3LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=g_D0CSbFXAGh6VV4tOwXgiOkgIonQFPjiqRtBpH19nQ=) | | [Donate →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119793678/584246240/320240526?refcode=email_112225_trumpresponse&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjU0MyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlM2VhMDk1MC1jOGM3LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=g_D0CSbFXAGh6VV4tOwXgiOkgIonQFPjiqRtBpH19nQ=)

Chris

name: Jason Crow
subject: Creed
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Colorado [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/Screenshot%202025-08-21%20at%2010.50.41%E2%80%AFAM.png]
Hi there, serving in the United States Army was one of the greatest honors of my life. I’ve never forgotten the creed I lived by when I served as an Army Ranger:

“My country expects me to move further, faster, and fight harder than any other.”
“I will shoulder more than my share of the task, whatever it may be, 100% and then some.”
“Under no circumstances will I ever embarrass my country.”

I served three combat tours in Iraq and Afghanistan.

My three tours taught me a lot about respect, working under pressure, and the value of selfless service to others.

As a leader, it was on me to set an example — to be the last one to eat, and the first one to jump out of the plane.

https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-nov25 [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-nov25]

I joined the military to protect our rights and freedoms. I ran for office to make a difference in people’s lives. Now more than ever, it’s clear: we need more leaders who are ready to do the hard work and bring people together.

My number one priority in Congress is to make life better for working people and protect our rights, but I can’t do it alone. Can you pitch in $10 right now? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-nov25?amount=10]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

ANOTHER AMOUNT [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-nov25]

Thank you,

Jason

name: Tim Walz
subject: Let’s make these 12 months count
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
The 2026 election will be here before we know it!
#
Time is flying by, Peter.
I can’t believe Thanksgiving is just a few days away. Then, the 2025 holiday season will really be in full swing, and before you know it, it’ll be 2026!
It made me realize we're less than one year away from Election Day in our reelection race for governor of Minnesota. That’s not a lot of time, so we’ve got to make the most of it.
**With our November end-of-month fundraising deadline coming up just one week from today, and so much work left for us to do, I have to ask:**
**[Will you sign up to make a monthly donation of any amount to fuel us through next November?](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251122_1%3Frecurring%3D1.C_Dmul&amount=5&%3Brefcode=20251122-145&t=1&akid=145%2E34582%2EhhDrkx)**
**[Donate Monthly ››](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251122_1%3Frecurring%3D1.C_Dmul&amount=5&refcode=20251122-145&t=2&akid=145%2E34582%2EhhDrkx)**
Early and consistent support is the most powerful thing you can provide to build a winning campaign. Monthly contributions of $5 or $10 may not sound like much, but over twelve months they really add up!
Plus, a recurring commitment allows our team to get a better sense of our budget and make plans to use every dollar wisely for events, TV and digital ads, and other efforts to reach voters across Minnesota.
[**Let’s give these next twelve months everything we’ve got: become a recurring donor to our people-powered campaign today!**](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251122_1%3Frecurring%3D1.C_Dmul&amount=5&%3Brefcode=20251122-145&t=3&akid=145%2E34582%2EhhDrkx)
I’m grateful,
Tim
P.S. If you can’t swing a monthly donation right now, I completely understand. **[A one-time contribution of any amount](https://act.timwalz.com/go/148?t=4&akid=145%2E34582%2EhhDrkx) **will still make an impact and help ensure we hit our fundraising goal before November ends.

**[Donate ››](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251122-145&t=5&refcode2=145_34582_hhDrkx&akid=145%2E34582%2EhhDrkx)**
---
[](https://timwalz4gov.org/JOIN)
Email us: [info@walzforgovernor.org](mailto:info@walzforgovernor.org)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.walzforgovernor.org/signup/receive-fewer-emails/)
* [Update My Personal Information](https://act.walzforgovernor.org/signup/did-your-information-change/?t=6&akid=145%2E34582%2EhhDrkx)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.walzforgovernor.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=7&akid=145%2E34582%2EhhDrkx)

Prepared and paid for by Walz for Governor
Tim Walz for Governor
P.O. Box 4337
St. Paul MN 55104

name: Team Marsha Blackburn
subject: I’m so sorry
body:
Your response was just flagged

**So far, only 99 have responded... _which means we’re just waiting on YOU!_**

Help me continue to show up in red districts, MAGA podcasts, and conservative spaces. It’s working. |

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119794709/584249173/2092395975?refcode=em-11.22.25-split-all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWM1ZmQ3MTMtY2RjNy1mMDExLTgxOTYtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1RgPXYBSs7RU3hdrMks9n9CNfsXYZXw2WH0rYZLoD-E=) Friend, I’m going to say something blunt: for the first time ever, **we have split the MAGA base, and we did it by doing what Democrats have refused to do for years**. **After much early opposition, the House voted 427-1 in support of my bill to release the Jeffrey Epstein files.** This didn’t happen by accident. It happened because I’ve spent months:
* going on MAGA-aligned podcasts
* holding town halls in deep-red districts
* building personal relationships across the aisle, like those with Rep. Massie and even MTG
* treating MAGA voters with respect — not mockery
* making the case for transparency and economic patriotism

Washington didn’t believe this strategy would work. But it did. It’s the most consequential crack in the MAGA coalition in nearly a decade – since Trump came down his golden escalator and announced his candidacy for President. And if we can break Trump’s coalition on transparency and a government for the people, not the rich, powerful, and well-connected, we can break it on other issues too and build a broad, durable, cross-partisan governing majority. But I need your help to keep showing up where Democrats never go. **[Will you rush a split of $3 to my campaign and Randy Villegas to help us keep going on conservative podcasts, holding town halls in Republican districts, and building the coalition we need to defeat extremism?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119794710/584249174/2092395975?refcode=em-11.22.25-split-all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWM1ZmQ3MTMtY2RjNy1mMDExLTgxOTYtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1RgPXYBSs7RU3hdrMks9n9CNfsXYZXw2WH0rYZLoD-E=)** | | [👉 CHIP IN NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119794711/584249175/2092395975?refcode=em-11.22.25-split-all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYWM1ZmQ3MTMtY2RjNy1mMDExLTgxOTYtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=1RgPXYBSs7RU3hdrMks9n9CNfsXYZXw2WH0rYZLoD-E=)

We don’t take lobbyist money. We don’t take corporate money.
Our campaigns are powered entirely by grassroots supporters like you.
Your contribution funds:
* **Travel and event logistics** to reach communities that beltway insiders ignore.
* **Promotion and organizing** to bring new people into the movement.
* **Security and operations** so we can keep doing this safely and sustainably.

Together, we can reshape the political landscape in ways Washington still doesn’t understand.
Thank you for your activism and all that you do.
In solidarity,
Ro Khanna

Meet Coach Tuberville:

**Coach Tuberville was born in Camden, Arkansas, the third of three children of Olive and Charles Tuberville. College Football 's 2004 Coach of the Year and two-time SEC Coach of the Year, Tommy Tuberville is most often recognized for his success on the field. But it is the legacy of his father, a highly decorated WWII veteran and Purple Heart recipient, that motivates Coach Tuberville to give back to the brave men and women of the United States Armed Forces.

In 1989 Coach Tuberville met his future wife, Suzanne Fette, at the Sugar Bowl in New Orleans. They were married in 1991 and have two boys, Tucker and Troy, who continue to make their dad proud.

In his career as a College Football Coach, Tommy started a long and successful career at the University of Miami, starting as a graduate assistant and rising to the Hurricanes' defensive coordinator. He led the Canes to three AP national championships and coached future Hall of Famers Ray Lewis and Warren Sapp, as well as Dwayne "The Rock" Johnson.

In 1998, Coach Tuberville was announced as the next head coach for the Auburn Tigers. Tuberville's time at Auburn was a success by any measure, as the Tigers captured eight consecutive bowl appearances, including five New Year's Day bowl appearances, five SEC Western Division titles, one SEC Championship, and a 13-0 season in 2004, after which Coach Tuberville was once again named the AP's SEC Coach of the Year. For his efforts during the perfect 2004 season, Tuberville would be named Paul "Bear" Bryant Coach of the Year, Associated Press Coach of the Year, Walter Camp Coach of the Year, Sporting News Coach of the Year, and AFCA Coach of the Year, among others.

_In 2020, Coach Tuberville became Senator Tuberville, winning his election to represent all Alabamians in the United States Senate with the full support of President Trump. Since then, he's supported President Trump's America-First policies while fighting off the Radical Left. In his first term as Senator, Tommy was one of eight Republicans to vote against certifying the 2020 election, has been a strong pro-life advocate, and called out Democrats for being pro-crime.

Now, Coach Tuberville is running for Governor so he can continue representing Alabamians and Americans in his homestate of Alabama. The Radical Left thinks they can flip Alabama blue and turn it into the California of the South. Can he count on your support? _

** [**HELP COACH TUBERVILLE**](http://links.tubervilleforalabama.com/u/click?_t=b8b4efbe79f64b579dfa7d8da685a7a9&_m=dce303237b0947f2bafe29674a2124cf&_e=Ur41gxhndWYggT97I8gBOYrBHXmPxjwLZf1zRUUd8AWixA5pSzve8p38M37UCfh8tP3DGH8SiGOh4XCkFZ00AZKMh-lhdzjtVqwBdlaGawBrNmcCBa5TOnOHMfBzt0fpyw9LEi1FbvC4rSRgoMajnvOcXU1Bs1z5cAP9kD7rR8MDxBq74pd-pqmcg8LiiDWU6f3B6KN_fGeAfVTkNQ1Bj60PMVnP1YluvFfcS_5nwkBU6kRZ3oIboIB-Fc3o5fbslesTEXIomBIsdT1Znon_9lrYvZxGDyDQkVFrIEKc6C1NgAO4H9P9ySQASFXRf5oEN-Z1uj8OqLP_UTUIlPmBee3FNlfigom0SYZkw8-D4KRuni6Y1PI75hKLajE9tO8J9o0qoxEOtrAHkuOfRagBOwUWI-OP_J0a_4rNiVnc1tA3Td2dfsbmPaDlgBuQKllcrgKP2INdYyjgGGoUxO1lAXqQbHkKnpG_BzFfFpD0eopwMY1S-KUHaeVRvthg9XrBWooOKrU7uK5TQGVINfAk6-XxIm-2hrhzzXScOkAMwKCn_FLnHMF-7PDTIq-5uae7Y3vfh8KcgTUp7EJjziCfhQ%3D%3D)

name: Team Global Reach
subject: Hostages. Wrongful detentions. You can make a difference.
body:
Americans held overseas need you. Will you step in?
Help bring American hostages and detainees home — add your voice to Team Global Reach.
|
---
Hi Friend,
---

---
We’re Global Reach, a team of independent hostage negotiators working with families of American hostages and wrongful detainees to bring their loved ones home — at no cost. This year alone, we’ve helped free 17 people in places like Kuwait, Venezuela, and Russia. We fight for the cases that slip through the cracks — the ones without headlines or a champion in Washington. Heather

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. name: Lizzie Fletcher
subject: Texas Map heads to SCOTUS
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
body:
ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/Non-Re-Elect%20Logo.png]
As I wrote to you earlier this week, a three-judge panel in El Paso federal court delivered an important victory for fair representation in Texas, blocking the mid-decade congressional map President Trump demanded and that Governor Abbott and Republican legislators provided—a map the court found was unconstitutional.
In a two to one decision, the Court did not mince its words. It stated directly, "The public perception of this case is that it’s about politics. To be sure, politics played a role in drawing the 2025 Map. But it was much more than just politics. Substantial evidence shows that Texas racially gerrymandered the 2025 Map." The Court has ruled that the Trump/Abbott map violates the Constitutional rights of Black and Hispanic Texans in violation of the 14th and 15th Amendments. They confirmed that the actions taken by Abbott and Legislature were driven by their desire to undermine the rights of Black and Hispanic Texans. Their claims of partisan motivations were rejected.
Texas Attorney General (and Senate candidate) Ken Paxton has already appealed, taking this fight to the U.S. Supreme Court, seeking an emergency stay of this ruling from the U.S. Supreme Court. Friday night, Justice Alito issued a temporary administrative stay and ordered the plaintiffs to respond to the motion by Monday at 5:00pm. The full Court will review filings and see arguments supporting the District Courts' ruling that the Trump/Abbott map is an illegal racial gerrymander.
Preparing the response to the motion to stay and to defend the injunction at the Supreme Court takes significant resources. The Texas Justice Fund has been at the forefront of this work to ensure that Texans have fair maps and a fair chance to be heard. As they prepare for the next hugely consequential stage of this battle, they need our support.
I’m making a contribution to the Texas Justice Fund today, and I’m asking you to join me. When you give through this email, every dollar will go directly to the Texas Justice Fund to support the legal work to present the strongest possible briefing to the Supreme Court. TJF can accept contributions in unlimited amounts. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpf_tjf?refcode=lpf_tjf_scotus]
DONATE TO TJF [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpf_tjf?refcode=lpf_tjf_scotus]
This appeal is the next step in a long fight for fair maps, fair representation, and free and fair elections. We cannot underestimate the determination of those who want to roll back these rights—but we also cannot underestimate what we can accomplish when we stand together.
Together we can do anything,
Lizzie Signature [https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP10/1/82676/images/lizzie_signature-1.png]

DONATE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lpf_tjf?refcode=lpf_tjf_scotus]

VOLUNTEER [https://secure.ngpvan.com/Ln8e8kjn2Euuh3daVlmozQ2]
3262 Westheimer, PMB 636, Houston, TX 77098
PAID FOR BY ELIZABETH PANNILL FLETCHER FOR CONGRESS
Don't want to receive these emails anymore? unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/yiT4vmtXrUCBvy-D5k60rg2?unsubscribedata=TzKw%2F19QAwcgpxUCg9WSbo3Gw68sL6NKewygScOTJ4kKEkiaFZQx9Rxa1Fgqh2%2BMOGmlnRVRC%2FiGfESVhpwF5f%2BLXhsol1GHxDJTG3USxNdIMsV0qvyB3Q8NJQ8Uyu0ckklpXCLTdA26IFUQgRGq6JT5%2FnX6V2%2BKxv7dv2xvYLb6PTdZqc3%2B19%2BMxYmCqRFWLTzVYQfJN79jZJt5ns1W5J9TEt0JQNAQswy1VT%2Bf3wa5L76hQZPpnoaz9f%2BgWEfYN1eJ4HyIM9Q0ceXAGy%2FlfoiivfjjWOxULE5Vxs85N8B4lO4OXEaycnIDiRnjxF4uX71HjDcu4yrl4m35x7voesZtaJeRuWRLfUDujYcRZcHBLuF5EJxqQfRbXS6GuKGo3oB%2BXUlLsZXKnfK28WquQdITvhLlq5kg1M2tUIqQZlCrmgwro3NZI8kxy3%2FLXcEo www.lizziefletcher.com [https://lizziefletcher.com/]

| | | | | |
# Checking in with Matt Cartwright

It was a top priority of mine as a congressman to restore Amtrak service to our area. I’m happy to report that these funds, which we announced at the end of last year, are coming in on schedule. The Federal Railroad Administration suffered substantial personnel cuts this year under “DOGE,” but is continuing its work on this project. Our area should not take a backseat to other regions that do have Amtrak service.
Earlier this month, I was humbled to be listed among the honorees receiving the Robert W. Munley Award from Lackawanna Pro Bono, which provides free legal services. Congratulations also to Hon. Chester Harhut, retired Common Pleas Court Judge, and Dr. Katie Pittelli, CEO of Johnson College. We raised over $100,000 at that event, money that will go toward LPB support staff and administrative expenses.
I am very grateful to the generous donors, as these funds help the needy and relieve our court system of the extra burden of handling unrepresented people. LPB showcases the generosity of our local legal community, who all pitch in, working for free. I was thrilled to help LPB and so honored to receive an award named for my late father-in-law, the man who taught me how to be a lawyer.
LPB can be reached at 570-961-2714 if you would like to make a donation, or you know someone in need.
I will keep you posted on what I’m working on. In the meantime, may you enjoy a Happy Thanksgiving, and let me know if I can lend a hand to you or someone you love.
Be safe and well,
Matt

---

name: Elise Stefanik
subject: I am officially running for governor of New York
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Elise for Governor
body:
You deserve to be among the FIRST to know.

You deserve to be among the FIRST to know.
| [](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zgd8hofpckbgi0m3mg9jr8k7fekd8fsic2tp26i0_rp22sh2s8i6eor6c3imap32cph2bs)
---
**_OFFICIAL ANNOUNCEMENT FROM REP. ELISE STEFANIK_**
---
Friend,
---
As one of my biggest grassroots supporters, I wanted to tell you this HUGE news personally...
---
**_I’m officially launching my campaign for governor of New York – and I’d like _YOU_ to be one of our very first Founding Members!_**
---

[ACCEPT YOUR
FOUNDING MEMBERSHIP >>](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zknsjl3a01kqiipbklrde4rla0sv1m7575kbveb0_rp22sh2s8i6eor6c3imap32cph2bs)
---

Kathy Hochul is the WORST governor in America, and she’s brought our once great state to its knees with her crippling taxes, crushing government regulations, and pro-criminal agenda.
---
__But her embrace of communist jihadi Zohran Mamdani was the FINAL straw.__
---
**As I watched the results come in on election night, I felt it was my duty to step up to the plate and fight with everything I have to SAVE New York from this communist takeover!**
---
This isn’t just another election, Friend.
---
**_It’s the _FINAL_ _BATTLE_ to SAVE New York before it’s lost forever!_**
---
Hochul will rubber stamp Mamdani’s dangerous communist agenda that seeks to abolish prisons, seize private property, drown us in taxes, let violent illegal aliens torment U.S. citizens, and tyrannically weaponize the power of the state against anyone who disagrees.
---
**But as the next Governor of New York, I will be the _FINAL_ _FIREWALL_ that protects New Yorkers from this insanity!**
---
We’re starting off in a strong position. Back-to-back polls just showed us with a _**ONE-POINT LEAD**_ – _and that’s before I even officially launched our campaign!_
---
As long as we raise the funds we need to fight back against Hochul’s attacks, cut through her multimillion-dollar ad buys, and get every last Republican to the polls, we have a clear path to victory.
---
That’s why the most important thing we can do right now is show a SURGE of grassroots support with thousands of Founding Members joining the ranks of our campaign.
---
Make no mistake, this will be the KEY to victory. Your support today could help us move one step closer to unlocking MILLIONS of dollars in matching funds from New York state!
---
**_That’s not a gimmick – it’s a REAL, authentic,_600% MATCH_!_**
---
When I’m sworn in as the next Governor of New York, I’ll know exactly who I have to thank...
---
**_Our Founding Members like _YOU_ who had my back on day one!_**
---
[**So please, make a contribution of ANY amount to become an official Founding Member of my gubernatorial campaign to FIRE Kathy Hochul – and help unlock a 600% MATCH.**](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2z8j18r3hur6b0t74dljjg446ni32s6vaol21krrg_rp22sh2s8i6eor6c3imap32cph2bs)
---

[ACCEPT YOUR
FOUNDING MEMBERSHIP >>](https://e.firstlook.news/rd/9z2zlrb7lkl9ta82s0qjt782sk81b3qpug4oh4uir88_rp22sh2s8i6eor6c3imap32cph2bs)
---

Thank you,
---

---
Rep. Elise Stefanik
Chairwoman of House Republican Leadership

body:
His record is full of political games and self-serving decisions that did nothing for the people he was supposed to represent.

| Peter,

Politico is reporting that Anthony D’Esposito may run for Congress again.

This is the same Anthony D’Esposito who has abused every position of power he has held. He has misled voters, cut corners and put himself first every time. His record is full of political games and self-serving decisions that did nothing for the people he was supposed to represent.

He has been attacking me online for months. Now he is not ruling out a rematch after losing by only two points last year.

[_**I need your help to show that we are ready for this fight and that this district will not be represented by someone who cannot be trusted. Will you contribute today to protect this seat?**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/119797137/584257747/800699251?refcode=BLU_EM_LGC_2025-11-22_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzMvMS85NzIwMiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI5MGQ2NTJjMy1kNGM3LWYwMTEtODE5Ni02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=pVp00xTsdxOwbVPh8Z157MBlzMhpt5328KTizT7_kMM=)

By Marilyn Kunce

# [Fake Colors vs Poison Seeds](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=179661708&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5NjYxNzA4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjM4MzgwMjEsImV4cCI6MTc2NjQzMDAyMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.sB6Tx-tUa6hYQg9UFW5BchIR4NwEsjWht0Sr9PlVnAk)
### By Marilyn Kunce
| | [Lucas Kunce](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---
| Nov 22
---
| [](https://substack.com/@lucastyree)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=179661708&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5NjYxNzA4LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MzgzODAyMSwiZXhwIjoxNzY2NDMwMDIxLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTg2Nzc0MSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.cUQedLii3Xdju7Oe5upMAJWWDOS7VoGd3diT3RdPars&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=179661708&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5NjYxNzA4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjM4MzgwMjEsImV4cCI6MTc2NjQzMDAyMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.sB6Tx-tUa6hYQg9UFW5BchIR4NwEsjWht0Sr9PlVnAk&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1867741&post_id=179661708&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc5NjYxNzA4LCJpYXQiOjE3NjM4MzgwMjEsImV4cCI6MTc2NjQzMDAyMSwiaXNzIjoicHViLTE4Njc3NDEiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.sB6Tx-tUa6hYQg9UFW5BchIR4NwEsjWht0Sr9PlVnAk)
---
| | [](https://substack..lPLbxHgsfN4dqlVfmDsjma88e3bHL61zXwfM9CI8WGY?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/lucaskunce/p/fake-colors-vs-poison-seeds?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

The other day, I scrolled past a headline that made me stop: _"[Walmart plans to cut synthetic dyes and additives from its store brands."](https://substack.com/redirect/1fca2b69-59db-4bd5-8e2f-9820c63af9ef?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)_
Boxes of the most known brand cereals lined the photo. And beneath it, the news that one of the world's largest corporations is bowing to political and consumer pressure: [no more Red 40, Yellow 5, Blue 1, or the cocktail of artificial dyes parents and scientists have warned about for years](https://substack.com/redirect/c9053609-dbdc-4180-b444-74f19b9e4ce7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
[Artificial food dyes are linked to behavioral difficulties in children](https://substack.com/redirect/7f3e82bc-4858-44cb-a24a-81556b93879b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and harm to memory and learning, allergies, and even potential carcinogenic effects under certain conditions. And there are plenty of foods we'd never suspect contain artificial food dyes but actually do, including some seemingly healthy foods. [Here](https://substack.com/redirect/d71c8cf1-a02c-4bee-9307-9b1b8a59800f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is a good source to read more on this, I found a few items I actually wasn't aware of their use of dyes.
So I was surprised to see Wal Mart moving in the right direction on this.
Although I really shouldn't have been. Corporations are all afraid of the MAGA+MAHA movement right now and the [writing has been on the wall for food dyes since RFK came in](https://substack.com/redirect/3d590c85-b9d2-46f3-a15e-194930f89bd4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). So Wal-Mart was really just doing what many corporations are doing right now: identifying and then doing the smallest things they can in order to fend off MAGA ire, while completely ignoring even larger public health issues that MAGA funders have quietly told political people not to worry about anymore.
**Because here 's the bitter irony**: while our breakfast bowls are being cleaned up of fake colors, [the very seeds that make our food remain coated in toxic chemicals that most of us never see,](https://substack.com/redirect/cece6f5d-703d-4a8e-a433-5c1a4d66a2d9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) never consented to, and that R[FK used to call poison](https://substack.com/redirect/cdb9fb09-09ae-4a2f-99a9-bfb25d16e50e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)-- until he joined an administration that was owned by the poison vendors, of course.
I guess for him selling out to big chemical companies is a small price to pay for the opportunity to potential eliminate vaccine regimes that save millions of American lives every year...
But I think we all need to know about these seed companies and chemicals regardless of who funds the folks in power, so I want to share with you what is happening there.
Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9sdWNhc2t1bmNlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.tcwDUmzkDpuFv7i1Msb9egRbCITCsGfIq1KZbG6ssys?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=179661708)
In short, the seed-industry has adopted a widespread practice of coating most of the seeds it sells with toxic chemicals, particularly a type known as Neonicotinoids. Just like with artificial colors, most of us don't know about this. And why would we? Mounting evidence shows that neonic seed treatments often [deliver little or no yield benefit](https://substack.com/redirect/fb106b35-e32c-4a8a-8c8d-233da821512e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in many cropping systems, so it's not like its some sort of broad societal success that we would have heard about. It's only real success, in fact, is that it's profitable and allows seed companies to charge more for their seeds by claiming that each kernel is an all-inclusive product.
It's the same story as food dyes. The food-industry slipped synthetic dyes into breakfast cereals under the radar because "kids like bright colors" the same way that the ag-industry slipped neonic coating into every kernel because "farmers need protection." The chemicals slipped into our daily lives, not because we demanded them, but because it was **profitable** to sell them that way. They exist because of _the culture of sale_.
Here in the Midwest, seeds don't come plain anymore. Corn, soy, and even vegetables are all coated in these neonics and fungicides before they ever hit the soil. And the coatings are supposed to provide invisible "protections" come at a steep cost: [bees and pollinators are collapsing, waterways are contaminated, soils degrade, farmworkers inhale dust, and farm families are exposed during planting.](https://substack.com/redirect/afeb259b-f51e-4a4f-b718-8beba13d355a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)
If one of America's biggest corporations is finally acknowledging that yes, we deserve food without hidden chemicals and is getting Red 40 out of cereal, why can't we get neonics out of seeds?
When I read the Walmart story, I remembered the work a colleague of mine at NRDC is working on, and decided to reach out to learn more about this issue. She's been working on sustainable ag, soil health, and seed reform on Colorado's fields, and by extension, what that might mean for the Midwest.
She explained exactly what these chemicals are.
Neonics are a class of synthetic, neurotoxic-active insecticides developed in the mid-1990s. They're now among the most widely used insecticides in the U.S. agricultural system.
They are systemic chemicals. Once applied, they travel through the plant's tissues, so leaves, pollen, and nectar, even dew, all bear traces of them.
In insects, neonics bind to nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (nerve-cell channels) and effectively lock them into over-drive. The insect's nerves fire continuously until the system collapses: tremors, paralysis, death. Because the binding is strong and not readily reversible in the insect nervous system, the harm is effectively permanent for that individual.
They are indiscriminate killers. pollinators and beneficial insects cannot avoid exposure simply by "staying away from sprayed fields." Neonics travel easily through soil and water, contaminating plants and waterways throughout agricultural areas. And even in tiny, "sublethal" doses, neonics interfere with insect learning, reproduction, navigation, and immunity.
In [2015, the U.S. Geological Survey study](https://substack.com/redirect/6c865264-dff5-453c-9175-d02d5a63be39?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) found neonic pollution in more than half of the streams it sampled nationwide, from agricultural regions to urban creeks. That was a decade ago. Given that use of treated seed has exploded since then, it's reasonable to assume the contamination footprint is even larger today.
Zoom in from the national picture to a single field, and the story gets even more revealing.
If you follow the trail of the science, it tells a simple story**: most of the neonics we coat on seeds never actually reach the pest they 're meant to control**. Instead they [stay behind in the soil, in the dust, and in the water](https://substack.com/redirect/fad9de4d-f68e-4eeb-b143-98e24aecc200?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Research following neonics through irrigated fields has found that residues persist in groundwater months after planting. Instead of vanishing up through the plant and into insects, much of the insecticides migrate downward and outward, and move through the ecosystem like runoff from an oil slick, one layer deeper with every rain.
Even inside the plant itself, most of the chemical doesn't stay for long. Tests on corn and cotton show that the concentration of neonics is almost 100 times higher in tiny seedlings than it is by the time the plant reaches maturity. In other words, the seed is drenched with insecticide at the very start of life, when pests aren't even a major threat, and by the time the plant might actually need protection, most of that chemical has already faded or washed away. What remains lingers not in the crop, but in the soil and the water, far from the parts of the plant that need protection, but directly in the line of fire for beneficial insects and others. a
And here's the part we rarely think about, what happens next? Those chemicals don't just disappear when they fade from the plant. From there, they work their way into the food chain, touching everything that feeds, drinks, or nests nearby.
Neonics don't stop with the bug they're meant to kill. They spread through the food chain--into the bodies of birds, fish, and pollinators--rippling through entire ecosystems. Studies tie their use to dramatic bird losses and fishery collapses, and in 2023 the EPA warned that [neonics are now pushing over 200 already-threatened species toward extinction](https://substack.com/redirect/c05d5653-6786-46f2-8f5d-0498e4ec60e4?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
For a Midwest farm, when you plant treated seed, you're introducing into the soil more than just germinating kernel and nutrients. You're introducing a chemical load that lives in the seed, transfers into the plant, drifts or leaches into the environment, and affects non-target insects (pollinators, beneficials), soil health, water bodies animals and even humans.
[Here is a video](https://substack.com/redirect/76b6c8e8-56dd-4b75-8419-51d3ba55061d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that explains further the toxic truth about neonics.
**What it means for American families**
When we shift from the field to the dinner table, the story of neonics becomes personal. This isn't just about farm policy or environmental science. It's about what ends up in our bodies and our children.
Neonics were designed to [act like nicotine](https://substack.com/redirect/cc650f18-735f-4a38-9fbd-4fe9b7f034a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), mimicking it inside an insect's nervous system. But that same mechanism affects mammalian brains and nerves, including our own. And these systems in animals and people are critical for healthy brain development, growth, and function.
Research has found that neonic exposures during pregnancy are linked to [heart](https://substack.com/redirect/4cc4dec2-f2fa-4bd5-ab95-7c125093b065?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [brain defects](https://substack.com/redirect/d3dc1f93-5434-47ae-a89a-6c870a391a7d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [autism-like symptoms](https://substack.com/redirect/a942e8ae-be0a-4d48-bfb7-d9cded3c3860?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [reduced cognitive abilities in children](https://substack.com/redirect/54ca363a-fb69-4ef9-aa0d-b18f98f24729?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). In adults, the effects are no less alarming: [lower testosterone](https://substack.com/redirect/57892b4a-e68e-40f7-a566-0feabcbd05c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [sperm counts](https://substack.com/redirect/f8d0db25-8e14-4c1b-8d6d-c9dacc856659?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), altered [insulin regulation](https://substack.com/redirect/06205f82-88ae-4293-a06c-ef1e35ccb83f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and [changes to fat metabolism](https://substack.com/redirect/62a838e8-857f-4489-adc0-8e5c256d19fc?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) have all been observed in populations exposed to neonics.
Today, neonics are measurable in roughly [half of the U.S. population](https://substack.com/redirect/6dac6e2e-4a1f-4c6e-9209-18dfccb611b6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) at any given time. Biomonitoring studies of pregnant women have detected neonics in over 95 percent of participants, _and those levels are still rising_.
And exposure isn't just from standing in a treated field. [Neonics regularly pollute drinking water](https://substack.com/redirect/e34185de-f015-42e1-9327-ad68292d175c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), where chlorination often fails to remove them and may even make the water more toxic. They also show up in the food we eat. Residues have been found in [86 percent of U.S. honey](https://substack.com/redirect/253b96f8-9d57-4841-80f5-16f0a394713e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and in [fruits and vegetables](https://substack.com/redirect/9f61583f-d6c7-4aff-9aa2-a87b0e07372f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), including many children's favorites like apples, cherries, strawberries, potatoes, and leafy greens. Because neonics are systemic, and come from the very seed the food comes from, they don't just sit on the surface; they're inside the fruit itself. You can't wash them off or peel them away.
While Europe has banned outdoor uses of the major neonic chemicals since 2018, and Canada has moved to significantly restrict some of their most widespread uses, little has changed in the United States. Several states are demanding to ban these pesticides, the latest in the list is Colorado, with their campaign for "[The Seed We Need](https://substack.com/redirect/ab93cec0-0681-49dc-ad4f-ef16b7efa843?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)". (Their website includes more info on how to make this change possible, make sure to visit it!)
And yet, because the chemical industry is a political powerhouse in America, strong enough to silence RFK, of all people, nothing changes here, no one talks about it, and we continue to unwittingly ingest poison while celebrating Wal-Mart doing something it was already going to have to do.
I'm writing this because I'm tired of the way that these big industries seem to silence their critics so easily and I want people to know. We all deserve better
Marilyn Kunce

clean_body: By Marilyn Kunce | | ---|---|--- | | | Forwarded this email? ? for more ? By Marilyn Kunce | | | Nov 22 | | | | | | | | | | | | The other day, I scrolled past a headline that made me stop: " Boxes of the most known brand cereals lined the photo. And beneath it, the news that one of the world's largest corporations is bowing to political and consumer pressure: . and harm to memory and learning, allergies, and even potential carcinogenic effects under certain conditions. And there are plenty of foods we'd never suspect contain artificial food dyes but actually do, including some seemingly healthy foods. is a good source to read more on this, I found a few items I actually wasn't aware of their use of dyes. So I was surprised to see Wal Mart moving in the right direction on this. Although I really shouldn't have been. Corporations are all afraid of the MAGA+MAHA movement right now and the . So Wal-Mart was really just doing what many corporations are doing right now: identifying and then doing the smallest things they can in order to fend off MAGA ire, while completely ignoring even larger public health issues that MAGA funders have quietly told political people not to worry about anymore. Because here 's the bitter irony: while our breakfast bowls are being cleaned up of fake colors, never consented to, and that R-- until he joined an administration that was owned by the poison vendors, of course. I guess for him selling out to big chemical companies is a small price to pay for the opportunity to potential eliminate vaccine regimes that save millions of American lives every year... But I think we all need to know about these seed companies and chemicals regardless of who funds the folks in power, so I want to share with you what is happening there. Lucas's Substack is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber. In short, the seed-industry has adopted a widespread practice of coating most of the seeds it sells with toxic chemicals, particularly a type known as Neonicotinoids. Just like with artificial colors, most of us don't know about this. And why would we? Mounting evidence shows that neonic seed treatments often in many cropping systems, so it's not like its some sort of broad societal success that we would have heard about. It's only real success, in fact, is that it's profitable and allows seed companies to charge more for their seeds by claiming that each kernel is an all-inclusive product. It's the same story as food dyes. The food-industry slipped synthetic dyes into breakfast cereals under the radar because "kids like bright colors" the same way that the ag-industry slipped neonic coating into every kernel because "farmers need protection." The chemicals slipped into our daily lives, not because we demanded them, but because it was profitable to sell them that way. They exist because of the culture of sale. Here in the Midwest, seeds don't come plain anymore. Corn, soy, and even vegetables are all coated in these neonics and fungicides before they ever hit the soil. And the coatings are supposed to provide invisible "protections" come at a steep cost: If one of America's biggest corporations is finally acknowledging that yes, we deserve food without hidden chemicals and is getting Red 40 out of cereal, why can't we get neonics out of seeds? When I read the Walmart story, I remembered the work a colleague of mine at NRDC is working on, and decided to reach out to learn more about this issue. She's been working on sustainable ag, soil health, and seed reform on Colorado's fields, and by extension, what that might mean for the Midwest. She explained exactly what these chemicals are. Neonics are a class of synthetic, neurotoxic-active insecticides developed in the mid-1990s. They're now among the most widely used insecticides in the U.S. agricultural system. They are systemic chemicals. Once applied, they travel through the plant's tissues, so leaves, pollen, and nectar, even dew, all bear traces of them. In insects, neonics bind to nicotinic acetylcholine receptors (nerve-cell channels) and effectively lock them into over-drive. The insect's nerves fire continuously until the system collapses: tremors, paralysis, death. Because the binding is strong and not readily reversible in the insect nervous system, the harm is effectively permanent for that individual. They are indiscriminate killers. pollinators and beneficial insects cannot avoid exposure simply by "staying away from sprayed fields." Neonics travel easily through soil and water, contaminating plants and waterways throughout agricultural areas. And even in tiny, "sublethal" doses, neonics interfere with insect learning, reproduction, navigation, and immunity. In found neonic pollution in more than half of the streams it sampled nationwide, from agricultural regions to urban creeks. That was a decade ago. Given that use of treated seed has exploded since then, it's reasonable to assume the contamination footprint is even larger today. Zoom in from the national picture to a single field, and the story gets even more revealing. If you follow the trail of the science, it tells a simple story: most of the neonics we coat on seeds never actually reach the pest they 're meant to control. Instead they . Research following neonics through irrigated fields has found that residues persist in groundwater months after planting. Instead of vanishing up through the plant and into insects, much of the insecticides migrate downward and outward, and move through the ecosystem like runoff from an oil slick, one layer deeper with every rain. Even inside the plant itself, most of the chemical doesn't stay for long. Tests on corn and cotton show that the concentration of neonics is almost 100 times higher in tiny seedlings than it is by the time the plant reaches maturity. In other words, the seed is drenched with insecticide at the very start of life, when pests aren't even a major threat, and by the time the plant might actually need protection, most of that chemical has already faded or washed away. What remains lingers not in the crop, but in the soil and the water, far from the parts of the plant that need protection, but directly in the line of fire for beneficial insects and others. a And here's the part we rarely think about, what happens next? Those chemicals don't just disappear when they fade from the plant. From there, they work their way into the food chain, touching everything that feeds, drinks, or nests nearby. Neonics don't stop with the bug they're meant to kill. They spread through the food chain--into the bodies of birds, fish, and pollinators--rippling through entire ecosystems. Studies tie their use to dramatic bird losses and fishery collapses, and in 2023 the EPA warned that . For a Midwest farm, when you plant treated seed, you're introducing into the soil more than just germinating kernel and nutrients. You're introducing a chemical load that lives in the seed, transfers into the plant, drifts or leaches into the environment, and affects non-target insects (pollinators, beneficials), soil health, water bodies animals and even humans. that explains further the toxic truth about neonics. What it means for American families When we shift from the field to the dinner table, the story of neonics becomes personal. This isn't just about farm policy or environmental science. It's about what ends up in our bodies and our children. Neonics were designed to , mimicking it inside an insect's nervous system. But that same mechanism affects mammalian brains and nerves, including our own. And these systems in animals and people are critical for healthy brain development, growth, and function. Research has found that neonic exposures during pregnancy are linked to and , , and . In adults, the effects are no less alarming: and , altered , and have all been observed in populations exposed to neonics. Today, neonics are measurable in roughly at any given time. Biomonitoring studies of pregnant women have detected neonics in over 95 percent of participants, and those levels are still rising. And exposure isn't just from standing in a treated field. , where chlorination often fails to remove them and may even make the water more toxic. They also show up in the food we eat. Residues have been found in , and in , including many children's favorites like apples, cherries, strawberries, potatoes, and leafy greens. Because neonics are systemic, and come from the very seed the food comes from, they don't just sit on the surface; they're inside the fruit itself. You can't wash them off or peel them away. While Europe has banned outdoor uses of the major neonic chemicals since 2018, and Canada has moved to significantly restrict some of their most widespread uses, little has changed in the United States. Several states are demanding to ban these pesticides, the latest in the list is Colorado, with their campaign for "". (Their website includes more info on how to make this change possible, make sure to visit it!) And yet, because the chemical industry is a political powerhouse in America, strong enough to silence RFK, of all people, nothing changes here, no one talks about it, and we continue to unwittingly ingest poison while celebrating Wal-Mart doing something it was already going to have to do. I'm writing this because I'm tired of the way that these big industries seem to silence their critics so easily and I want people to know. name: Sherrod Brown
subject: We’re facing an uphill battle, and I need folks like you by my side to make it to the top.
body:
I know you’re likely getting a ton of emails from candidates and causes right now, Peter.
But I have to ask: Is there anything I can say to convince you to chip in $25 between Real Texas PAC and my Senate campaign in Ohio? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
What if I told you that right after I joined this race, experts at The Cook Political Report increased our odds of winning?
What if I told you that CNN and The New York Times report that Ohio is “in play” – but that corporate special interests are gearing up to spend millions against me?
What if I told you that misleading attack ads have already hit the airwaves – and that whether we’re able to counter these smears and reach voters with the truth depends on people like you?
This grassroots, small-dollar donor movement is our best chance to flip Ohio’s Senate seat. But we’re facing an uphill battle, and I need folks like you by my side to win.
So please, split $25 or more between Real Texas PAC and me to power our campaign forward, win in Ohio, and keep up the fight for working people. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/rtp-2025-sherrod?refcode=101725_2]
If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation to Sherrod Brown and The Real Texas PAC will go through immediately:
